cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: Treasure AI Named a Leader in 2026 IDC MarketScape for AI-Enabled CDPs (B2C and B2B)
stories.plain: The 2026 IDC MarketScape places Treasure AI in the Leader tier for AI-enabled CDPs across both B2C and B2B segments — a rare double ranking. This matters because it signals that buyers and analysts now expect CDPs to natively power agentic experiences and real-time personalization, not just store data, shifting procurement criteria across the category.

stories.head: How to Build a Hermes Agent-Style Workflow That Cuts AI Data Waste
stories.plain: This practical guide shows marketers how to redesign agentic AI workflows so models receive only the data they actually need, reducing cost and latency without losing capability. For teams buying or building marketing AI, it is a concrete architectural pattern — not theory — that directly affects how agents are scoped and governed in production.

stories.head: Heimdal Report: Big Gap Between Executive AI Confidence and Operational Risk Reality in 2026
stories.plain: A survey of 1,000 IT professionals in the US and UK finds executives believe AI is well-governed while practitioners on the ground report significant control gaps. For MarTech buyers, this disconnect is critical: it means AI systems running marketing automation may carry unmanaged risk that compliance and legal teams have not yet been asked to audit.

stories.head: Sybal and Carahsoft Make AI Governance Tools Available to US Government Agencies via SEWP
stories.plain: Sybal's Proof of Governance solution is now procurable by US government agencies through Carahsoft's SEWP contract vehicle. This is not a logo deal — it opens a formal procurement path for AI governance software in the public sector, accelerating the spread of explainability and audit requirements that will likely influence commercial MarTech standards too.

stories.head: The Hidden Cost of a Fragmented Martech Stack: How Best-of-Breed Tools Build a Complexity Wall
stories.plain: This analysis argues that assembling many specialist tools creates a structural complexity wall that slows revenue operations more than it unlocks capability. As agentic AI requires tightly integrated data flows across the stack, the piece reframes the build-vs-consolidate debate — directly relevant to anyone evaluating CEP or CDP purchases right now.

stories.head: How to build a Hermes Agent-style workflow
stories.plain: Most agentic AI workflows dump far too much data into the model, raising cost and reducing accuracy. This practical guide shows how a Hermes Agent-style architecture filters context before it hits the model — a design pattern that directly affects how marketing teams buy, configure, and govern their agentic AI systems.

stories.head: Expert Mode: From Personalization to Agentic Transformation
stories.plain: A senior practitioner piece tracing the shift from rules-based personalization to agentic decisioning — systems that choose channel, message, timing, and offer per person autonomously. It explains why two decades of CDP and segmentation investment is now the foundation for a fundamentally different operating model, changing what marketers need to buy and build next.

stories.head: Identity Legibility Assumes the Agent Can Reach You
stories.plain: Drawing on Harvard Business Review research, this piece argues that AI agent optimization — making your brand's data complete and trustworthy enough for an autonomous shopping agent to recommend you — is becoming a prerequisite for commerce visibility. It reframes GEO from an SEO tactic into a structural data quality problem that affects how brands are built.

stories.head: AI visibility depends on who writes about your brand
stories.plain: New analysis shows that strong search rankings do not translate into AI answer citations — what drives AI visibility is third-party editorial coverage from sources large language models trust. This changes how marketing teams should allocate PR and content budgets and introduces measurable new KPIs for generative engine optimization.

stories.head: Heimdal: AI Risk Management in 2026 — Bridging the Enterprise Control Divide
stories.plain: A survey of 1,000 IT professionals in the US and UK finds a major gap between executive confidence and practitioner reality on AI risk controls. For marketing and CX leaders deploying AI at scale, the report identifies governance, policy clarity, and technical safeguards as the urgent missing layer — directly shaping how AI is audited and procured in enterprises.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: Agentic AI is reshaping the workflow, not just the tool
body: The conversation has moved decisively from AI as a feature inside tools to AI as an autonomous workflow orchestrator. Pieces on Hermes-style agent pipelines and the shift from personalization to agentic transformation show practitioners being asked to redesign process logic, not just plug in a chatbot.

S&P Global restructuring its operating model around agentic solutions — and appointing new executives to lead it — signals that enterprise org charts are being redrawn around this shift. The strategic question is no longer 'which AI vendor?' but 'who owns the orchestration layer?'
ev: MarTech Series · S&P Global agentic pivot

themes.head: Trust, identity & AI governance as moat
body: Multiple items converge on the idea that AI capability is now a commodity — the durable competitive advantage belongs to brands that solve identity legibility, governance, and trust. The Agile Brand futurist piece states it bluntly: 'AI is table stakes; trust, identity, and orchestration are the moat.'

This is backed by concrete moves: Sybal-Carahsoft partnering on AI governance for the public sector, Heimdal publishing an enterprise AI-risk-management framework, and a separate piece noting that an agent can only reach you if your identity infrastructure lets it. Marketers who ignore the plumbing will find their agents blocked at the door.

themes.head: Fragmented stacks & bad data erode AI returns
body: Three distinct pieces hammer the same underlying warning: AI amplifies data quality problems rather than hiding them. Fragmented martech stacks carry hidden costs that compound when AI systems are layered on top, and bad training data actively misdirects ad spend at scale.

The Madison Logic-Harris Poll finding — that nearly half of B2B marketers admit they are guessing at purchase drivers — quantifies the data-confidence deficit. Before optimizing AI models, teams need to audit whether the signals feeding those models are trustworthy in the first place.
ev: MarTech · Hidden cost of fragmented stack
MarTech · Bad data wastes ad budget

themes.head: AI-generated creative becomes the primary targeting lever
body: As cookie-based and demographic targeting erodes, creative itself is becoming the targeting signal. AI tools that generate and test thousands of creative variants at speed mean the message — not the audience segment — is increasingly what does the segmentation work.

This is reinforced by the rise of AI-powered video-recreation tools for e-commerce and the recognition of AI-enabled CDPs in the IDC MarketScape. Brands that treat creative production as a strategic data asset, feeding performance signals back into generation loops, will outpace those still treating creative as a cost center.
ev: Agile Brand · Treasure AI CDP leader

themes.head: AI visibility now depends on third-party editorial authority
body: A quietly consequential shift: how AI models surface your brand is determined by what authoritative third parties have written about you, not by your owned content or SEO metadata. This rewrites the rules of brand visibility and elevates earned media, analyst citations, and trade-press coverage as first-order marketing infrastructure.

Paired with HubSpot's Warmly acquisition pointing toward AI-native CRM that monitors intent signals in real time, this suggests that the entire awareness-to-conversion funnel is being re-intermediated by AI layers. Marketers need a presence strategy for AI answer engines, not just search engines.
ev: MarTech · AI visibility & who writes about you
MarTech · Stop adopting AI, solve problems

month.takeaway: Across every layer of the stack, AI is shifting competitive advantage from who has the best tools to who has the cleanest data, the most trusted identity infrastructure, and the strongest third-party authority — making plumbing and provenance the new marketing strategy.
